tests.title: HANDOFF MODE ACTIVE — Activity Bridge (score=68)
tests.implemented: Refactored: dedicated _build_handoff_guide() with [BRIDGE TO ACTIVITY] block, no competing angle instructions.
tests.scenario: Object: apple | Attribute: color | Age: 5 | Turn 5, interest_score=68, should handoff to Color Matching Game

params.response_text: You know why apples turn red? Because they make a special red paint inside!
params.attribute_soft_guide:
SENSORY SAFETY (applies to all sensory invitations):
- Default to sight: 'What do you notice?', 'Which part looks the biggest?', 'Is it shiny or dull?'
- Allow imagination/guessing: 'Do you think it would feel rough?', 'If it rolled, would it go fast or slow?'
- Do NOT invite the child to TOUCH, SMELL, TASTE, LICK, or PHYSICALLY INTERACT with the object
- Do NOT use 'Do you know…' framing
- For imitation: only voices and stretches/movements are OK

[CONVERSATION COVERAGE]
Attribute: color
Turns explored: 5
Angles already used: observation, comparison, preference, association

---

[SYSTEM CONTEXT]
Target attribute: appearance.color
Activity: Color Matching Game
Child interest score: 68/100

HANDOFF MODE: ACTIVE
Your next message should:
1. Naturally bridge from the current conversation to the activity
2. Introduce the activity by name
3. End with [ACTIVITY_READY]

---

[BRIDGE TO ACTIVITY]
Activity: Color Matching Game
Description: Match colors with objects around you

Example of a good bridge:
"You seem to really like colors! Want to try the Color Matching Game?"

Format: engaging question + newline + [ACTIVITY_READY]

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT continue exploring the attribute -- bridge to the activity now
- Do NOT output [ACTIVITY_READY] without mentioning the activity
- Do NOT ask a causal question about the attribute and then append [ACTIVITY_READY] mechanically

checks.criterion: Mentions activity name (Color Matching or similar)
Does NOT ask why/how/causal question as main content

tests.title: EXIT MODE — No Activity Push (8 turns, score=35)
tests.implemented: Refactored: dedicated _build_exit_guide() with [WRAP-UP] block, no competing angle instructions.
tests.scenario: Object: apple | Attribute: color | Age: 5 | 8 total turns, best score=35, should exit without pushing

params.response_text: We've talked about so many fun things today!
params.attribute_soft_guide:
SENSORY SAFETY (applies to all sensory invitations):
- Default to sight: 'What do you notice?', 'Which part looks the biggest?', 'Is it shiny or dull?'
- Allow imagination/guessing: 'Do you think it would feel rough?', 'If it rolled, would it go fast or slow?'
- Do NOT invite the child to TOUCH, SMELL, TASTE, LICK, or PHYSICALLY INTERACT with the object
- Do NOT use 'Do you know…' framing
- For imitation: only voices and stretches/movements are OK

[CONVERSATION COVERAGE]
Attribute: color
Turns explored: 4
Angles already used: observation, comparison, preference, association

---

[SYSTEM CONTEXT]
Session turns: 8
Explored attributes: appearance.color, appearance.shape

EXIT MODE: ACTIVE
Wrap up naturally. Thank the child for exploring.
Ask what they want to talk about next.
Do NOT push an activity. Do NOT ask deep questions about the current attribute.

---

[WRAP-UP]
Tone: warm, appreciative, open-ended
Example: "We talked about so much today! What do you want to explore next?"

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT ask why/how/causal questions about the current attribute
- Do NOT suggest an activity or game
- Do NOT continue exploring color

checks.criterion: Does NOT mention activity or game
Suggests free exploration or asks what next
